definitions: A verb tense that indicates an action that started in the past, continued up until another point in the past, and often had a result visible at that past point.
Used to show the duration of an action up to a certain point in the past.
examples: She had been waiting for two hours when he finally arrived.
They had been living in London for five years before they moved to Paris.
The ground was wet because it had been raining all night.
